schema.appDescription: Calculate maximum home loan amount and property price you can afford based on your income, existing EMIs, and down payment.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Home Affordability Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: How much home loan can I get in India?
faq.a: Banks typically lend 75–80% of property value (loan-to-value ratio). Maximum EMI = 40–50% of net monthly income minus existing EMIs (FOIR norms). Example: ₹1 lakh net income, existing EMIs ₹15,000, max new EMI = ₹35,000. At 8.5% for 20 years, max loan = approximately ₹33.4 lakh.

faq.q: How much of my income should go to home EMI?
faq.a: Financial advisors recommend EMI not exceeding 30–35% of gross income (or 40% of net take-home). Higher EMI commitment limits your ability to build an emergency fund, invest for retirement, and handle income disruptions. Don't stretch beyond 40% FOIR even if the bank approves.

faq.q: What is the ideal down payment percentage for a home?
faq.a: RBI mandates banks lend minimum 10–25% of property value (maximum LTV 75–80%). Make the largest down payment you can without depleting emergency fund. Down payment of 25–30% reduces loan EMI, total interest, and avoids PMI. Use accumulated savings, EPF withdrawal (after 5 years), and gifts.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹1 lakh net income — how much home can I afford?
examples.text: Net income ₹1 lakh, existing EMIs ₹15,000, available down payment ₹15 lakh. Max new EMI (40% FOIR): ₹25,000. Loan at 8.5% for 20 years: ₹23.87 lakh. Plus ₹15 lakh down payment = ₹38.87 lakh property. In Mumbai, this buys a 1BHK in suburban areas.

examples.heading: Impact of income increase on affordability
examples.text: Same person gets a 20% raise (new income ₹1.2 lakh). Max new EMI rises to ₹33,000. Max loan: ₹31.5 lakh. With ₹15 lakh down payment: ₹46.5 lakh property. A 20% income increase translates to ~20% higher affordability.
